abort_reason: operator_relogin_was_a_FALSE_CONFIRMATION; live 8787 was the 04-23 zombie gateway, not a fresh restart
stop_line_triggered: gateway 需要再次 relogin，但我不在場 (overnight stop line #6)
actions_taken_after_abort: did NOT call any mutating endpoint

checks.interpretation_after_forensics: STALE — the kgi_logged_in=true flag was module-level cache from the 04-23 15:24 login; KGI upstream heartbeat was actually dead since 04-24 06:00 (Q021 max reconnection attempts reached)

checks.result: presumed 200 (no error logged before script crash)
checks.interpretation: likely returned cached account from 04-23; KGI not actually queried because upstream was dead
module-level state mutation only; no real KGI side effect because session was zombie
likely accepted by stale state but no actual subscription registered upstream (heartbeat dead)

checks.post_diagnostic: gateway 8787 LISTEN socket gone; netstat shows only TIME_WAIT remnants; no python uvicorn bound to 8787

forensic_evidence.heartbeat_thread_dead_since: approx 2026-04-24 06:02 — log shows continuous 'heartbeat thread not connected' loop ever since
forensic_evidence.404_root_cause: uvicorn loaded app.py at 04-23 15:24, before W1.5 endpoints were added; in-memory FastAPI app does not have /position /trades /deals routes
forensic_evidence.gateway_process_state_now: dead — no LISTEN on 8787, only TIME_WAIT entries

root_cause_chain: 1. Operator's runbook Step A (stop old gateway via Ctrl+C) likely did not actually kill the 04-23 zombie — or operator stopped a different terminal.

2. Operator's runbook Step B (start new uvicorn) likely failed silently with [Errno 10048] address already in use — operator's console may have shown the startup banner from a stale window OR the new process exited immediately.

3. Operator's runbook Step C (POST /session/login) hit the 04-23 zombie gateway, which returned 200 because module-level state still had logged_in=true cached — but the actual KGI upstream session was dead since 04-24 06:02.
4. Elva's pre-script /health check read the same stale flag and believed relogin succeeded.

5. The 04-23 zombie's app.py does not have /position /trades /deals routes (loaded before Lane B work today). My script's call to /position would have hit 404, but the gateway process appears to have crashed mid-request before responding.
required_operator_actions_morning: 1. Confirm whether new uvicorn console is still showing 'waiting for POST /session/login' or has exited; if stale, close it.
2. Verify no python.exe still bound to 8787 (Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 8787); kill any stragglers (PID 27408 / 31552 may be unrelated runners — check before killing).
3. Do a CLEAN restart: cd services/kgi-gateway, uvicorn app:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8787 --log-level info — confirm bind via the 'Uvicorn running on' line BEFORE attempting login.
4. After bind confirmed, run /session/login from operator's local secret holder.
5. Validate fresh state: GET /health should show {kgi_logged_in:true, account_set:false}; GET /position should NOT be 404 (route exists in current app.py).
6. Then say 'W2a relogin 完了，跑 read-side verify' and Elva will retry.
lessons_to_codify: Pre-flight check 'kgi_logged_in=true' is INSUFFICIENT signal — must also verify a W1.5 endpoint exists (e.g. GET /position not 404) to confirm the running uvicorn loaded the current app.py.
Operator's restart-success confirmation must include 'Uvicorn running on' message visible in the new console, not just the startup banner.
Add a /version or /build_info endpoint in app.py that returns a content hash — Elva can verify the running gateway matches expected build before driving any read-side test.
elva_signature: W2a aborted under stop line #6; awaiting operator clean restart
